title: Converting between 12-hour and 24-hour time and finding elapsed time
problem: A cinema session starts at $7{:}45\text{ pm}$ and ends at $9{:}50\text{ pm}$. Write both times in 24-hour format and find the duration of the session in minutes.

steps.instruction: Convert the start time from 12-hour to 24-hour format.
steps.working: For pm times from $1{:}00\text{ pm}$ onwards, add 12 to the hour. $7 + 12 = 19$, so $7{:}45\text{ pm} = 19{:}45$.

steps.instruction: Convert the end time from 12-hour to 24-hour format.
Calculate the elapsed time in minutes.
steps.working: From $19{:}45$ to $21{:}45$ is $2$ hours $= 120$ minutes. From $21{:}45$ to $21{:}50$ is $5$ minutes. Total duration $= 120 + 5 = 125$ minutes.

title: Converting between Australian time zones and expressing UTC offsets
problem: It is $4{:}30\text{ pm}$ AEDT (UTC+11) in Sydney. Find the current time in Perth (AWST, UTC+8) and state the equivalent UTC time.

steps.instruction: Convert Sydney AEDT to UTC by subtracting the UTC offset.
steps.working: AEDT is UTC+11, so subtract 11 hours. $16{:}30 - 11 = 05{:}30$ UTC.

steps.instruction: Convert UTC to Perth time by adding Perth's UTC offset.
steps.working: AWST is UTC+8, so add 8 hours. $05{:}30 + 8 = 13{:}30$. Perth time is $1{:}30\text{ pm}$ (13:30).

steps.instruction: State the UTC time and summarise.
steps.working: The UTC time is $05{:}30$. Sydney (AEDT, UTC+11) is $3$ hours ahead of Perth (AWST, UTC+8), since $11 - 8 = 3$.

title: Solving a multi-timezone flight problem using UTC
problem: A flight departs Auckland (NZST, UTC+12) at $9{:}00\text{ am}$ on Saturday and takes 11 hours to reach Dubai (UTC+4). What is the local Dubai arrival time?

steps.instruction: Convert the Auckland departure time to UTC.
steps.working: NZST is UTC+12, so subtract 12 hours. $09{:}00 - 12 = -03{:}00$, which means $21{:}00$ (9:00 pm) on Friday UTC. (Going back 12 hours from 9:00 am Saturday crosses midnight.)

steps.instruction: Add the flight duration to find the arrival time in UTC.
Convert the UTC arrival time to Dubai local time.
steps.working: Dubai is UTC+4, so add 4 hours. $08{:}00 + 4 = 12{:}00$ Saturday. The flight arrives at noon local time in Dubai.

answer: The flight arrives at $12{:}00\text{ pm}$ (noon) on Saturday in Dubai.
